calendar.matters:
First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Apr-09-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn. John Harrison for Denise Smith (Respondnet).

Mr. Keith stated that Ms. Smith filed for bankruptcy, that the claim is directly against Ms. Smith in an individual capacity and that he filed a Notice of Stay of Proceedings. In addition, Mr. Keith stated that the stay is in effect to this proceeding and requested that the First Amended Petition to Compel The Trustee to Redress Breach of The Trust, etc. go off calendar. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Mar-12-2012 Continued To Apr-09-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn (Petitioner).

Mr. Keith stated that he learned about the Petitioner's death last week, that he advised Mr. Harrison (Attorney for Respondent) and requested a 30-day continuance. The matter was continued to April 9, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the Amended Petition for Instructions to Compel Trustee to Redress Breach of Trust, etc.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Jan-09-2012 Continued To Mar-12-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn.

Mr. Keith stated that he was waiting for subpeonaed records from Adult Protective Services and Kaiser. The matter was continued to March 12, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and status of mediation.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Oct-24-2011 Continued To Jan-09-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Harrison for Respondent. Philip Keith for Petitioner.

The Parties indicated that they are willing to participate in mediation after discovery is concluded. The matter was continued to January 9, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Jun-27-2011 Continued To Oct-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Harrison for Denise Smith (Respondent). Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn (Petitioner).

Mr. Keith stated that he received responses to the interrogatories, that both sides are about to subpoenae records from Adult Protective Services and that depositions need to be taken. The matter was continued to October 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and possible trial setting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Jun-20-2011 Continued To Jun-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-01-2011 Off Calendar

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Apr-25-2011 Continued To Jun-20-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Harrison for Denise Smith (Respondent). Philip Keith for Petitioner.

Responses to discovery have not been provided. The matter was continued to June 20, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Keith is to bring a discovery motion.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Apr-04-2011 Continued To Apr-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn. John Harrison for Denise Smith.

Mr. Harrison stated that he intends to file a responsive pleading to the petition and responses to the interrogatories within 30 to 45-days. Counsel stated that they were amenable to mediation. The Court will send the Parties to mediation once discovery is completed. The matter was continued to April 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Harrison's response to the First Amended Petition to Compel the Trustee to Redress Breach of Trust, etc. is to be filed by the end of the day April 20, 2011. Mr. Keith may bring a Motion to Compel Responses to Interrogatories if needed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

Petition For Instructions To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-01-2011 Off Calendar

First Amended Petition To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee; And To Set Aside First Amendment To Trust For Fraud And Undue Influence And For Elder Financial Abuse.
Off Calendar Feb-28-2011 Continued To Mar-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Continued To Mar-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Petition, Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Philip Keith
Off Calendar Jan-07-2011 Text Ruling

Petition For Instructions To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-30-2010 Continued To Jan-12-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-28-2010 Continued To Nov-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn. John Harrison for Denise Smith.

Mr. Keith requested putting the matter over stating that he had not been able to get together with his Client to commence discovery due to her medical condition/hospitalization. In addition, Mr. Keith stated that he had not received the Trust document, that he received the amendment to the Trust, expressed concern about execution of the amendment and that he needed to amend his petition. The Court raised the issue of notice. Mr. Harrison stated that his Client has attempted to contact Mr. Keith's Client and agreed to a continuance. The matter was continued to November 30, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosada/A. Payne.

Petition For Instructions To Compel The Trustee To Redress Breach Of The Trust; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Continued To Sep-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Harrison for and with Denise Smith (Respondent). Philip Keith for Loreen Quinn.

Mr. Harrison stated that the response was filed this morning. Mr. Keith requested a continuance. The matter was continued to September 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
